title: pi-astack settings
description: Configuration schema for pi-astack extensions. Lives at ~/.pi/agent/pi-astack-settings.json. Each top-level key namespaces one extension; missing sections fall back to that extension's hardcoded DEFAULTS. To enable IDE autocomplete + validation, add "$schema": "./path/to/pi-astack-settings.schema.json" as the first field in your settings file.

$schema.description: Path or URL to this schema file. Used by IDEs for validation and autocomplete; ignored at runtime.

_comment.description: Free-form comment field. JSON has no native comments; use this (or any underscore-prefixed key) for inline documentation.

modelCurator.description: Whitelist + capability hints for chat models. The curator filters pi-ai's hundreds of built-in model definitions to a curated keep-list per provider, then injects a markdown capability table into the main session system prompt on every turn.

providers.description: Keep-list per provider. Keys are provider names (anthropic, openai, deepseek, ...); values are arrays of model IDs to retain. All other models in that provider are removed from the registry. Providers not listed here are left untouched.

additionalProperties.description: Model IDs to keep for this provider

hints.description: One-line capability hints shown next to each chat model in the system prompt table. Keys are 'provider/modelId'. Style: present tense, max ~15 words, name a concrete strength so the LLM can reason about role fit.

examples.anthropic/claude-opus-4-7: Strongest reasoning; security audits, architecture critique. Highest cost.
examples.openai/gpt-5.5: Strong general-purpose reasoning + coding. Image input.

imageGen.description: Image generation models. NOT registered as pi Models (they can't do chat) — listed in a separate 'Image generation' section of the system prompt as informational hints only. Keys are model IDs (no provider prefix).

examples.gpt-image-2: Latest OpenAI image generation model. Call `imagine(prompt: "...", size?, quality?, style?)` to generate images.

modelFallback.description: Multi-model fallback chain with retry (extensions/model-fallback/). Asymmetric policy: the **initial** model gets pi's full retry budget before fallback kicks in (retry count auto-read from pi settings.json#retry.maxRetries, no separate config needed); **each fallback model** gets exactly 1 attempt — any error switches to the next configured model immediately. When all fallback models are tried and all fail, the extension stops. For claude-code parity (10 attempts, 1s-256s delays), set pi settings.json#retry.maxRetries=9 + retry.baseDelayMs=1000; the extension auto-aligns.

fallbackModels.description: Ordered list of `provider/modelId` strings. When the initial model exhausts its retries, the extension walks this list in order, picking the first un-tried entry that exists in pi's modelRegistry and has configured auth. Entries missing from the registry or without auth are logged as warnings and skipped. The currently-active model (whatever it is when the error happens) is auto-added to the tried set, so it's safe to include it in the list. Empty list = retry behavior only, no fallback.

sediment.description: Project-only sediment writer settings (extensions/sediment/). Current slice provides writer substrate only: sanitize + lint + lock + atomic markdown write + audit + best-effort git commit. Automatic LLM extraction is not enabled yet.

enabled.description: Enable agent_end sediment hook. Default false until automatic extraction is implemented.

gitCommit.description: Whether writer attempts best-effort git add + git commit after markdown atomic write. Git failure does not roll back markdown.

defaultConfidence.description: Default confidence for writer smoke/deterministic provisional entries.

minWindowChars.description: Minimum rendered run-window size. Smaller windows are skipped by future extractor logic.

maxWindowChars.description: Maximum rendered run-window size. The builder keeps the newest entries within this budget.

maxWindowEntries.description: Maximum number of newest session entries considered for a sediment run window.

extractorModel.description: Model used by /sediment llm --dry-run. Expected format: provider/modelId. This dry-run command does not write memory or advance checkpoint.

extractorMaxRetries.description: Max retries for /sediment llm --dry-run model call. Default 0 to avoid hidden repeated extraction costs.

extractorMaxCandidates.description: Quality gate for /sediment llm --dry-run: outputs with more candidates than this are marked too_many_candidates.

extractorAuditRawChars.description: Number of raw LLM output characters stored in the llm_dry_run audit preview. SHA-256 is stored for full raw output when present.

autoLlmWriteEnabled.description: Master switch for the LLM auto-write lane in the agent_end hook. When false (default), sediment runs only the deterministic explicit-marker extractor. When true AND `/sediment readiness` reports ready (sufficient dry-run samples + pass rate), the hook will additionally invoke the LLM extractor on transcript windows where no MEMORY: block was found. All auto-written entries are subject to the autoWrite* gates below in addition to the standard dedupe/sanitize/lint pipeline.

minDryRunSamples.description: Minimum llm_dry_run audit samples required before automatic LLM writing may be enabled.

requiredDryRunPassRate.description: Required pass rate over llm_dry_run samples before automatic LLM writing may be enabled.

autoWriteForceProvisional.description: When the LLM auto-write lane fires, coerce the entry's `status` to `provisional` regardless of what the LLM proposed. Defends against the LLM auto-creating an `active`/`archived` entry that should require human curation. Strongly recommended to leave true; relaxing this widens the LLM's authority over sediment state transitions.

autoWriteDisallowMaxim.description: Reject any LLM auto-write candidate with `kind: maxim`. Maxims are the highest authority entry kind; we never want an LLM minting them. Strongly recommended to leave true.

autoWriteDisallowArchived.description: Reject any LLM auto-write candidate whose `status` is `archived`, `deprecated`, or `superseded`. These transitions should follow citation/curation flows, not appear at first creation.

autoWriteMaxConfidence.description: Cap on `confidence` for LLM auto-written entries. Confidence in [7, 10] is reserved for human-attested entries that have survived peer review or repeated citation. Default 6 keeps auto-written entries clearly second-tier.

compactionTuner.description: Trigger pi's compaction at a configurable percentage of contextWindow (extensions/compaction-tuner/). Solves the problem that pi's built-in `compaction.reserveTokens` is an absolute number, while user-stack models span 200k–1M+ contextWindow — a single number can't represent a fixed percentage uniformly. Hooks `agent_end`, reads `ctx.getContextUsage()`, and calls `ctx.compact()` once `percent >= thresholdPercent`. Pi's built-in threshold (default reserveTokens=16384, ~92% on 200k) remains as a last-resort safety net. Audit log: `<projectRoot>/.pi-astack/compaction-tuner/audit.jsonl`.

enabled.description: Master switch. When false the hook is loaded but short-circuits before any work, leaving pi's native compaction in sole control.

thresholdPercent.description: Target percent of contextWindow consumed at which to fire `ctx.compact()`. Clamped to [10, 95]. Re-evaluated each turn; changes apply immediately on next agent_end with no restart needed.

rearmMarginPercent.description: Hysteresis margin (percentage points). After triggering at thresholdPercent, the trigger disarms until usage drops below `thresholdPercent - rearmMarginPercent`, then re-arms. Prevents repeated triggers while a long agent loop hovers near the boundary or while compaction is still finishing.

notifyOnTrigger.description: Show a one-line `ctx.ui.notify` message when compaction is triggered (and again on completion / error). The audit row is always written regardless of this flag.

customInstructions.description: Optional custom instructions passed through to pi's compaction LLM (`compact({customInstructions})`). Empty string = pass undefined and use pi's default summary prompt.

memory.description: Read-only markdown memory Facade (extensions/memory/). Registers memory_search/get/list/neighbors over project .pensieve/ and optional world ~/.abrain/ stores. The extension never writes memory; sediment remains the sole writer.

includeWorld.description: Whether memory_* read tools should also scan ABRAIN_ROOT (default ~/.abrain) when it exists. Missing world store degrades to project-only without error.

defaultLimit.description: Default result count for memory_search and memory_list.

maxLimit.description: Hard cap on per-call result count.

maxEntries.description: Maximum markdown files scanned per store to keep read tools bounded.

projectBoost.description: Internal ranking multiplier for current-project results. Not exposed in memory_search output.

shortTermTtlDays.description: Dry-run migration policy for legacy .pensieve/short-term/* entries: planned lifetime.expires_at = created + shortTermTtlDays. The read-only extension only reports this plan; it does not write files.

vision.description: Vision tool model selection (extensions/vision/). Auto-selects the strongest vision-capable model from the registry, excluding the caller's own model.

modelPreferences.description: Ordered preference list, most-preferred first. Each entry is 'provider/idSubstring' — substring match on model id (case-insensitive, version-suffix-tolerant). Models matching earlier entries win; unmatched models still participate, ordered by cost.input descending as a rough capability proxy.
